cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to analyze this ethical dilemma where an employee at a pharmaceutical company has to decide whether to stay quiet about a harmful side effect of a new drug or to speak out and risk their job. Let me start by breaking down the problem. First, the company has strict rules against sharing confidential information. That's part of their policy, probably to protect proprietary research and maintain competitive advantage. But then the employee discovers a potentially harmful side effect.
